objective: Separate output-alphabet and symbolic-tree enforcement from model training quality.
conclusion: Compositional output is not a valid proxy for symbolic constrained decoding. Lexer-native tokens are required. The symbolic compiler now recognizes BOS root, consumes partial grammar-admitted paths, suppresses legacy repair, and never appends an unconstrained suffix in tree/restricted mode. E165 still fails model-quality parse/structure gates, but its zero seeded fallbacks confirms the deterministic layer is actually applied; remaining failures are incomplete/low-quality model choices, not mixed unconstrained output.
